title: 10 possible side effects of Chrysanthemum morifolium (Ju Hua) if used improperly
intro: Chrysanthemum morifolium, also known as the garden mum, is a popular ornamental plant with potential medicinal properties. However, if used improperly, it can have several negative health effects. Firstly, consuming large amounts of the plant can cause stomach discomfort, nausea, and diarrhea. Secondly, it can lead to skin irritation and eye inflammation when handling the plant without protective gloves or when the plant's sap comes into contact with the eyes. Moreover, people who are allergic to members of the asteraceae/compositae family (which includes chrysanthemums) may experience allergic reactions such as skin rashes, respiratory issues, or anaphylaxis in severe cases. Lastly, Chrysanthemum morifolium may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, and can exacerbate pre-existing medical conditions, particularly those affecting the liver and kidneys. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using Chrysanthemum morifolium for medicinal purposes.
